prose:
**Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene — condensed Fukui indices (dimensionless, from Mulliken charges)**

**Method-provenance block**
- **Property:** Condensed Fukui functions (f⁺ electrophilic, f⁻ nucleophilic, f⁰ radical) and dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻), computed via the finite-difference / frozen-geometry charge-difference scheme using Mulliken (GFN2-xTB) partial charges of the neutral, cation, and anion.
- **Level of theory:** GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding method; program: xtb). No basis set and no functional apply to this Hamiltonian. GFN2-xTB is appropriate for geometries/conformers and relative reactivity trends, not chemical-accuracy absolute energies.
- **Geometry provenance:** User-supplied structure /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz (21 atoms; 2 C₅H₅ rings + Fe). Single-point charge calculations at this fixed geometry — the geometry was NOT re-optimized (frozen-geometry Fukui protocol).
- **Solvent / environment:** Gas phase (solvent = null; no implicit solvation).
- **Charge / multiplicity:** Neutral reference charge 0, multiplicity 1 (as requested). Cation: charge +1, multiplicity 2. Anion: charge −1, multiplicity 2 (both open-shell doublets).
- **Software:** xtb (GFN2-xTB); Mulliken population scheme.

**Headline results**
- **Most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻):** Fe (atom index 11, 1-based) with **f⁻ = 0.25938191**. This is the reported headline value.
- **Most electrophilic site (largest f⁺):** Fe (atom index 11, 1-based) with f⁺ = 0.22885456.
- **Dual descriptor at Fe:** −0.030527354 (negative → net electrophilic character in the dual-descriptor sense, though Fe carries the largest condensed f⁻ and f⁺ of any single atom). The C and H atoms show small positive dual-descriptor values (~+0.0015).

The iron center dominates both the electrophilic (f⁺) and nucleophilic (f⁻) condensed Fukui indices, consistent with the frontier density being largely metal-centered in this GFN2-xTB description.

**Convergence / integrity:** Engine integrity status = "ok", trustworthy = true. All three integrity checks passed (ok=true). The charge-conservation checks confirm sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f⁻) = 1.0000, indicating the N±1 states converged properly.

- "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"

(These three "warning"-severity checks all returned ok=true; they are the descriptive detail fields of the integrity checks and are surfaced here for completeness.)
